tagline: Onboards, screens and monitors customers — a BSA Officer signs every SAR and high-risk approval.
problem: Financial-crime compliance is a $61B labor sink of manual screening and alert triage — and a missed SAR or sanctions hit is personal liability for the BSA Officer.
outcome: Onboarding, screening and alert triage run straight-through with a documented rationale; only genuine risk reaches the BSA Officer, who signs every SAR and high-risk approval.

steps.does: Onboard the customer: verify identity and beneficial ownership
Screen the party + UBO against sanctions / PEP / adverse-media (continuous)
Monitor transactions, triage alerts, and draft the SAR narrative
The BSA/AML Officer signs the SAR filing and any high-risk onboarding
File the SAR with FinCEN and apply the onboarding decision
Maintain the monitoring loop and document dispositions for exam
